_meta.name: Default English Pack
_meta.author: solace-browser
_meta.description: 20 jokes + 20 facts + 10 greetings for the default English experience

jokes.text: Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs.
A SQL query walks into a bar, walks up to two tables and asks: 'Can I join you?'
How many programmers does it take to change a light bulb? None — it's a hardware problem.
Why do Java developers wear glasses? Because they can't C#.
There are only 10 types of people: those who understand binary and those who don't.
A programmer's kid asks: 'Dad, why is the sky blue?' Dad: 'It works on my machine.'
I told my wife she should embrace her mistakes. She hugged me.
Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything.
I'm reading a book about anti-gravity. It's impossible to put down.
Debugging: Being the detective in a crime movie where you are also the murderer.
Git commit -m 'fixed stuff'. The honest history of every project.
The best thing about UDP jokes? I don't care if you get them.
Why was the math book sad? It had too many problems.
My code never has bugs. It just develops random features.
A byte walks into a bar looking very pale. Bartender: 'What's wrong?' Byte: 'I feel a bit off.'
Why do AI models make great friends? They always listen and never judge — mostly.
I asked my AI assistant to tell me a joke. It said: 'Error 404: humor not found.' Then laughed at itself.
The cloud is just someone else's computer. The edge is just someone else's house.
CSS is so easy. You just need to center a div. *breaks into tears*
99 little bugs in the code, 99 little bugs. You take one down, patch it around — 127 little bugs in the code.

facts.text: Honey never spoils. Archaeologists found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible.
There are more possible chess games than atoms in the observable universe.
The first computer bug was an actual bug — a moth found in Harvard's Mark II computer in 1947.
The average person spends about 6 months of their lifetime waiting for red lights to turn green.
Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and can edit their own RNA in real-time.
The human brain generates about 70,000 thoughts per day.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
The speed of light is so fast that a photon takes only 8.3 minutes to travel from the Sun to Earth.
Bananas are berries botanically, but strawberries are not.
The average person walks about 100,000 miles in their lifetime — equivalent to 4 trips around Earth.
Python was named after Monty Python, not the snake.
The world's first website (info.cern.ch) is still online today.
SHA-256 produces 2^256 possible hashes — more than atoms in the observable universe.
The universe is about 93 billion light-years in diameter, but only 13.8 billion years old.
A single strand of human DNA, if uncoiled, would be about 6 feet long. All DNA in your body would stretch to the Sun and back 600 times.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the building of the Great Pyramid.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way galaxy.
Crows can recognize human faces and hold grudges against people who treated them badly.
The Greenland shark can live over 500 years, making it the longest-lived vertebrate known.
Your brain is more active when you're asleep than when you're watching TV.

greetings.text: Good morning! Ready to tackle the day?
Rise and shine! Your tasks are waiting.
Good afternoon! How's the day going?
Afternoon! Keep up the great work.
Evening! Hope the day was productive.
Welcome back! What shall we automate today?
Hey there! Ready to save some time?

seasonal.greeting: Happy New Year! Wishing you an amazing year ahead.
Spring is here! Fresh starts and new automations.
Summer vibes! Keep cool and automate more.
Fall is beautiful. So are well-organized workflows.
Winter warmth to you! Stay cozy and automate.
